Patch Set 2:

Just curious, if you combine this with your other patch and my "unaligned arithmetic" fix, what happens with 47K reads like i/o 500?

I know it still can't really handle that correctly, but I think this should still help...?

Patch Set 2:

> Just curious, if you combine this with your other patch and my
 > "unaligned arithmetic" fix, what happens with 47K reads like i/o
 > 500?
 > 
 > I know it still can't really handle that correctly, but I think 
 > this should still help...?

I think it should help, but I haven't got numbers for yet, I will post
if I get some.

Patch Set 2:

BTW, nice work here.  I find the readahead code almost impossible to follow ;)

Patch Set 2:

Passed enforced test review-zfs on CentOS 7.0/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-40vm6: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/0b53da77-c39d-49a7-9d6a-853ca8541bf9 ran 7 tests.

Patch Set 2:

I think we should definitely add a quick test doing strided readahead and checking misses - We have some today (sanity 101 family), but they only look at discards, not misses, so they never caught that strided readahead was broken.

It might just be enough to add miss checking to those tests?

commit.subject: LU-12043 llite: don't miss every first stride page
commit.message:
LU-12043 llite: don't miss every first stride page

Whenever we need skip some pages for stride io read, we
will calculate next start page index, however, this page
index is skipped every time, because loop start from index + 1

Testing command: iozone -w -c -i 5 -t1 -j 2 -s 100m -r 1m -F data
Without patch: 587384.69 kB/sec

                        read                    write
pages per rpc         rpcs   % cum % |       rpcs   % cum %
1:                      16  19  19   |          0   0   0
2:                       0   0  19   |          0   0   0
4:                       0   0  19   |          0   0   0
8:                       0   0  19   |          0   0   0
16:                      0   0  19   |          0   0   0
32:                      0   0  19   |          0   0   0
64:                      0   0  19   |          0   0   0
128:                     0   0  19   |          0   0   0
256:                     0   0  19   |          0   0   0
512:                    22  26  46   |          0   0   0
1024:                   44  53 100   |          0   0   0

With patch: 744635.56 kB/sec
                        read                    write
pages per rpc         rpcs   % cum % |       rpcs   % cum %
1:                       0   0   0   |          0   0   0
2:                       0   0   0   |          0   0   0
4:                       0   0   0   |          0   0   0
8:                       0   0   0   |          0   0   0
16:                      0   0   0   |          0   0   0
32:                      0   0   0   |          0   0   0
64:                      0   0   0   |          0   0   0
128:                     0   0   0   |          0   0   0
256:                     0   0   0   |          0   0   0
512:                     8  13  13   |          0   0   0
1024:                   50  86 100   |          0   0   0

We get better performances ~27% up here, and all 1 page RPC
disappear.

Change-Id: I126674cbe15197f0abdff256fdde3fc0c49c6898
